title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream, a band of wind in the middle latitudes, influences weather patterns worldwide.
This summer's heat waves, from Boston to Barcelona, are linked by the jet stream's wave patterns.
Researchers are studying 'quasi-resonant amplification', a possible cause of prolonged weather extremes.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots offensive line improved in 2025, investing in young talent like rookies Will Campbell and Jared Wilson.
New England ranked sixth in pass-block efficiency and second in explosive plays despite struggles in postseason with 21 sacks allowed.
Concerns remain about the offensive line's continuity and durability, especially with position changes and returning players recovering from injuries.

title: New mural brings fresh life to the corner of Doyle’s and the Midway Cafe
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A mural featuring Barack Obama and Anthony Bourdain is now at the corner of former Doyle's Cafe and Midway Cafe.
Commissioned by Lee Goodman of Watermark Development, the mural aims to revitalize the area affected by Doyle's 2019 closure.
Artist Lee Beard, known as SOÉMS, painted the mural, which includes quotes from Obama and Bourdain.

title: Largest known bottlenose dolphin stranding hits Cape Cod
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Up to 30 bottlenose dolphins were stranded on Cape Cod, with several dying while others refloated as the tide returned.
Rescue crews monitored and assisted 19 dolphins found in Wellfleet, deploying satellite tags for tracking and group reunification.
IFAW advises public to avoid moving stranded dolphins, citing increased stranding events in recent years near Cape Cod's tidal zones.

title: Personal ties are enhancing the tall ships experience at Sail Boston
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Approximately 12 out of nearly 60 vessels at Sail Boston 2026 offer charter passenger sails for a waterfront view.
The Nova Scotia tall ship Bluenose II, popular at Sail Boston, symbolizes historical ties to Boston post-1917 Halifax explosion.
The Spirit of Bermuda, a traditional sloop, highlights deep New England-Bermuda connections during the Sail Boston festival.

title: ‘I can’t believe that just happened’: Noah Kahan celebrated a hole-in-one at Boston Golf Club
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Noah Kahan hit a hole-in-one on the 11th hole at Boston Golf Club, using a five iron.
After performing four sellout shows at Fenway Park, Kahan shared the hole-in-one video on social media.
Kahan celebrated the achievement during an event with Keegan Bradley at George Wright Golf Course in Hyde Park.

title: Boston rents are down again. See how much the average apartment now costs.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's average rent dropped to $2,930, a decrease from $3,054 last year, marking 13 months of decline.
The vacancy rate increased to 3.2% as over 37,000 new units were added since 2021 amid lagging demand.
Employment in the Boston metro area decreased by 2% over the past year, impacting the rental market.

title: Mass. teen injured jumping off 64-foot N.H. waterfall
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old from Massachusetts was injured after jumping off a 64-foot waterfall in New Hampshire.
The teen landed on rocks at Glen Ellis Falls in Pinkham Notch, resulting in significant but non-life-threatening injuries.
Conservation officers and responders transported him to a hospital after he was found unable to walk.

title: Man allegedly damages ‘several’ cars with wooden stick and cinder block in East Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: A Roxbury man was arrested for allegedly damaging cars with a wooden stick and cinder block in East Boston.
Several parked cars near 279 Sumner St. had windows smashed and mirrors damaged on Sunday afternoon, police said.
27-year-old Revanth Kumar Mahesh faces arraignment in East Boston District Court on charges related to the vandalism.

title: Local obituary: Louisa Gag, 36, lifelong Boston resident and safe streets advocate
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Louisa Gag, 36, lifelong Boston resident and safe streets advocate, died on July 9 in a cycling accident.
Gag worked at Boston Transportation Department, contributing to Blue Bikes and street safety initiatives.
Memorial donations suggested to Livable Streets Alliance in Gag's memory; details of service pending.
